speaker: DUCHESS
text: My lord, you told me you would tell the rest,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: When weeping made you break the story off

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Of our two cousins coming into London.

speaker: YORK
text: Where did I leave?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: At that sad stop, my lord,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Where rude misgoverned hands from windows’ tops

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Threw dust and rubbish on King Richard’s head.

speaker: YORK
text: Then, as I said, the Duke, great Bolingbroke,

speaker: YORK
text: Mounted upon a hot and fiery steed,

speaker: YORK
text: Which his aspiring rider seemed to know,

speaker: YORK
text: With slow but stately pace kept on his course,

speaker: YORK
text: Whilst all tongues cried “God save thee, Bolingbroke!”

speaker: YORK
text: You would have thought the very windows spake,

speaker: YORK
text: So many greedy looks of young and old

speaker: YORK
text: Through casements darted their desiring eyes

speaker: YORK
text: Upon his visage, and that all the walls

speaker: YORK
text: With painted imagery had said at once

speaker: YORK
text: “Jesu preserve thee! Welcome, Bolingbroke!”

speaker: YORK
text: Whilst he, from the one side to the other turning,

speaker: YORK
text: Bareheaded, lower than his proud steed’s neck,

speaker: YORK
text: Bespake them thus: “I thank you, countrymen.”

speaker: YORK
text: And thus still doing, thus he passed along.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Alack, poor Richard! Where rode he the whilst?

speaker: YORK
text: As in a theater the eyes of men,

speaker: YORK
text: After a well-graced actor leaves the stage,

speaker: YORK
text: Are idly bent on him that enters next,

speaker: YORK
text: Thinking his prattle to be tedious,

speaker: YORK
text: Even so, or with much more contempt, men’s eyes

speaker: YORK
text: Did scowl on gentle Richard. No man cried “God save him!”

speaker: YORK
text: No joyful tongue gave him his welcome home,

speaker: YORK
text: But dust was thrown upon his sacred head,

speaker: YORK
text: Which with such gentle sorrow he shook off,

speaker: YORK
text: His face still combating with tears and smiles,

speaker: YORK
text: The badges of his grief and patience,

speaker: YORK
text: That had not God for some strong purpose steeled

speaker: YORK
text: The hearts of men, they must perforce have melted,

speaker: YORK
text: And barbarism itself have pitied him.

speaker: YORK
text: But heaven hath a hand in these events,

speaker: YORK
text: To whose high will we bound our calm contents.

speaker: YORK
text: To Bolingbroke are we sworn subjects now,

speaker: YORK
text: Whose state and honor I for aye allow.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Here comes my son Aumerle.

speaker: YORK
text: Aumerle that was;

speaker: YORK
text: But that is lost for being Richard’s friend,

speaker: YORK
text: And, madam, you must call him Rutland now.

speaker: YORK
text: I am in parliament pledge for his truth

speaker: YORK
text: And lasting fealty to the new-made king.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Welcome, my son. Who are the violets now

speaker: DUCHESS
text: That strew the green lap of the new-come spring?

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Madam, I know not, nor I greatly care not.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: God knows I had as lief be none as one.

speaker: YORK
text: Well, bear you well in this new spring of time,

speaker: YORK
text: Lest you be cropped before you come to prime.

speaker: YORK
text: What news from Oxford? Do these jousts and triumphs hold?

speaker: AUMERLE
text: For aught I know, my lord, they do.

speaker: YORK
text: You will be there, I know.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: If God prevent not, I purpose so.

speaker: YORK
text: What seal is that that hangs without thy bosom?

speaker: YORK
text: Yea, lookst thou pale? Let me see the writing.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: My lord, ’tis nothing.

speaker: YORK
text: No matter, then, who see it.

speaker: YORK
text: I will be satisfied. Let me see the writing.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: I do beseech your Grace to pardon me.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: It is a matter of small consequence,

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Which for some reasons I would not have seen.

speaker: YORK
text: Which for some reasons, sir, I mean to see.

speaker: YORK
text: I fear, I fear—

speaker: DUCHESS
text: What should you fear?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: ’Tis nothing but some bond that he is entered into

speaker: DUCHESS
text: For gay apparel ’gainst the triumph day.

speaker: YORK
text: Bound to himself? What doth he with a bond

speaker: YORK
text: That he is bound to? Wife, thou art a fool.—

speaker: YORK
text: Boy, let me see the writing.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: I do beseech you, pardon me. I may not show it.

speaker: YORK
text: I will be satisfied. Let me see it, I say.

speaker: YORK
text: Treason! Foul treason! Villain, traitor, slave!

speaker: DUCHESS
text: What is the matter, my lord?

speaker: YORK
text: Ho, who is within there? Saddle my horse!—

speaker: YORK
text: God for his mercy, what treachery is here!

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Why, what is it, my lord?

speaker: YORK
text: Give me my boots, I say! Saddle my horse!—

speaker: YORK
text: Now by mine honor, by my life, by my troth,

speaker: YORK
text: I will appeach the villain.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: What is the matter?

speaker: YORK
text: Peace, foolish woman.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I will not peace!—What is the matter, Aumerle?

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Good mother, be content. It is no more

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Than my poor life must answer.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Thy life answer?

speaker: YORK
text: Bring me my boots!—I will unto the King.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Strike him, Aumerle! Poor boy, thou art amazed.—

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Hence, villain, never more come in my sight.

speaker: YORK
text: Give me my boots, I say.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Why, York, what wilt thou do?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Wilt thou not hide the trespass of thine own?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Have we more sons? Or are we like to have?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Is not my teeming date drunk up with time?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And wilt thou pluck my fair son from mine age

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And rob me of a happy mother’s name?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Is he not like thee? Is he not thine own?

speaker: YORK
text: Thou fond mad woman,

speaker: YORK
text: Wilt thou conceal this dark conspiracy?

speaker: YORK
text: A dozen of them here have ta’en the sacrament

speaker: YORK
text: And interchangeably set down their hands

speaker: YORK
text: To kill the King at Oxford.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: He shall be none. We’ll keep him here.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Then what is that to him?

speaker: YORK
text: Away, fond woman! Were he twenty times my son,

speaker: YORK
text: I would appeach him.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Hadst thou groaned for him as I have done,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Thou wouldst be more pitiful.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: But now I know thy mind: thou dost suspect

speaker: DUCHESS
text: That I have been disloyal to thy bed

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And that he is a bastard, not thy son.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Sweet York, sweet husband, be not of that mind!

speaker: DUCHESS
text: He is as like thee as a man may be,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Not like to me or any of my kin,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And yet I love him.

speaker: YORK
text: Make way, unruly woman!

speaker: DUCHESS
text: After, Aumerle! Mount thee upon his horse,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Spur post, and get before him to the King,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And beg thy pardon ere he do accuse thee.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I’ll not be long behind. Though I be old,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I doubt not but to ride as fast as York.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: And never will I rise up from the ground

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Till Bolingbroke have pardoned thee. Away, begone!
